senses.definition: to strongly and sincerely state that a particular statement or belief is true

examples.x: In the wedding ceremony, the couple affirmed their commitment to each other with heartfelt vows.
The witness affirmed the accuracy of their account during the court proceedings.
The scientist affirmed the validity of the experimental results in the research paper.
The student affirmed the importance of education in shaping one's future during the graduation speech.

senses.definition: to broadcast something or be broadcast on TV or radio

examples.x: The network aired the new episode of the popular TV show last night.
The radio station aired an interview with the author discussing her latest book.
The local news channel aired live coverage of the city council meeting.
The sports network aired the championship game between the two rival teams.
The documentary film will be aired on public television next week.

senses.definition: (of news) to be released or made known to the public

examples.x: The news of the election results broke on live television.
The scandal finally broke after months of rumors.
The celebrity's engagement news broke on social media.
When the announcement of the new product broke, it generated a lot of excitement among consumers.
As soon as the details of the data breach broke, the company took immediate action to address it.

senses.definition: to express one's opinion about something or someone

examples.x: After reading the article, she decided to comment on the author's perspective and add her own insights.
During the meeting, participants were invited to comment on the proposed changes to the project plan.
Listeners were encouraged to call in and comment on the radio show, sharing their opinions on the discussed issues.
During the debate, each candidate had the opportunity to comment on their opponent's stance on various issues.
She didn't hesitate to comment on the new policy during the team meeting, expressing her concerns about its potential impact.

senses.definition: to write stories, articles, etc. for a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: She regularly contributes articles to the local newspaper.
He was invited to contribute a feature story to the magazine.
Many freelance writers contribute to various publications.
She hopes to contribute more frequently to the magazine’s lifestyle section.
The journalist was excited to contribute her first piece to the new online platform.

senses.definition: to refuse to admit the truth or existence of something

examples.x: The accused continued to deny any involvement in the theft, despite the evidence.
The student had to deny cheating on the exam, insisting on the fairness of their answers.
Despite the witness accounts, the defendant chose to deny any wrongdoing in court.
He continued to deny the allegations despite the overwhelming evidence.
She had to deny any involvement in the incident to protect her reputation.

senses.definition: to explain something thoroughly and with specific information

examples.x: In the report, the researcher detailed the methodology used in the experiment, ensuring transparency and reproducibility.
The detective carefully detailed the sequence of events leading to the crime, creating a clear timeline for investigation.
The car manual is designed to detail the proper maintenance procedures, ensuring the longevity of the vehicle.
During the presentation, the speaker will detail the key features and benefits of the new product line.

senses.definition: to share something between a large number of people

examples.x: The charity organization distributes food to families in need every week.
The company distributes its products to stores across the country.
The government recently distributed pamphlets to raise awareness about public health issues.
The publisher distributes books to libraries and bookstores for sale.
Can you distribute the worksheets to students before the class starts?

senses.definition: to give information about someone or something, especially in an official manner

examples.x: The teacher informed the students about the upcoming exam schedule and its format.
The news anchor will inform the public of the latest developments in the ongoing investigation.
The manager informed the team of the changes in the project timeline to keep everyone updated.
The sign in the park informs visitors about the rules and regulations for maintaining a clean environment.
The doctor took the time to inform the patient of the potential side effects of the prescribed medication.

senses.definition: to contain or include something as a necessary part

examples.x: Any investment involves an element of risk.
I didn’t realize putting on a play involved so much work.
The job involves my travelling all over the country.
The process involves using steam to sterilize the instruments.
The test will involve answering questions about a photograph.

senses.definition: the profession of collecting and editing pieces of news and articles either to be published in a newspaper, magazine, etc. or broadcast

examples.x: She studied journalism to become a reporter.
Journalism plays a crucial role in keeping the public informed.
The course covers various aspects of journalism, including writing and ethics.
He pursued a career in journalism after graduating from college.

senses.definition: an organization that gathers news stories for newspapers, TV, or radio stations

examples.x: The news agency sent reporters to cover the breaking story.
The news agency provided updates on the international crisis.
She worked for a major news agency covering political events.
The news agency is known for its reliable and fast news delivery.
The news agency’s report was picked up by newspapers around the world.

senses.definition: a system of delivering television programming to subscribers, using wires under the ground

examples.x: Cable television offers subscribers access to hundreds of channels, including premium and specialty networks.
Many households rely on cable television for news, entertainment, and sports programming.
The cable television industry has evolved with the introduction of digital and high-definition channels.
Subscribers can choose from various cable television packages that cater to different viewing preferences.
Cable television providers offer on-demand services and DVR options for recording and watching programs at a convenient time.

senses.definition: the reporting of specific news or events by the media

examples.x: The news coverage of the event was extensive and detailed.
She praised the newspaper for its thorough coverage of the election.
The journalist provided in-depth coverage of the humanitarian crisis.
The magazine’s coverage of fashion trends was always up-to-date.
The radio station's coverage of local sports is popular among listeners.

senses.definition: a piece of news that is not true or confirmed

examples.x: She warned her friends about the dangers of spreading fake news.
The article was quickly debunked as fake news by several fact-checkers.
The website was criticized for publishing fake news and misleading information.
Fake news can easily spread on social media platforms.
They held a workshop to teach people how to identify fake news.

senses.definition: a radio or TV presenter who is in charge of giving information about different programs

examples.x: The announcer introduced the guest speakers at the event.
She worked as an announcer for the local radio station.
The announcer’s voice was clear and engaging during the live broadcast.
At the sports game, the announcer provided play-by-play commentary.
He started his career as an announcer before moving into television reporting.

senses.definition: a person whose job is to talk on radio or TV programs or to present them

examples.x: The broadcaster delivered the evening news with great professionalism.
She became a well-known broadcaster after years of working in radio.
The broadcaster provided live updates from the scene of the event.
His role as a broadcaster includes interviewing guests and presenting news.
The broadcaster’s voice is familiar to many listeners in the area.

senses.definition: the department of a newspaper or a TV or radio station that is in charge of collecting and reporting the news

examples.x: The news desk handled all incoming reports and updates for the evening broadcast.
She called the news desk to confirm the details of the breaking news.
The news desk is busy coordinating with reporters across the city.
At the news desk, they quickly verified the information before publishing.
He worked at the news desk, managing the coverage of major events.

senses.definition: a group of people with special skills or knowledge who have been brought together to discuss, give advice, or make a decision about an issue

examples.x: The panel of experts discussed the challenges facing the education system.
The conference included a panel with leading scientists in the field.
The panel provided valuable advice on improving public health policies.
During the debate, the panel answered questions from the audience.
The panel's recommendations will help shape the new regulations.

senses.definition: a type of TV or radio program on which famous people appear as guests to answer questions about themselves or other subjects

examples.x: She appeared on a talk show to discuss her new book.
The talk show featured interviews with several celebrities.
His favorite talk show airs every evening at 7 PM.
They watched the talk show to hear the latest entertainment news.
A live audience attended the talk show to interact with the guests.

senses.definition: a meeting during which a very important person talks to journalists and answers their questions or makes a statement

examples.x: The company held a news conference to announce their new product.
The president scheduled a news conference to address the recent crisis.
Journalists from various outlets attended the news conference.
During the news conference, the spokesperson answered questions from the media.
She prepared several questions for the upcoming news conference.

senses.definition: a humorous drawing on the topics that are covered in the news, usually published in a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: Each issue features a cartoon that comments on current events.
The newspaper's cartoon poked fun at the recent political debate.
The editorial cartoon made light of the latest sports controversy.
She collects cartoons that reflect political and social issues.
Cartoons often use satire to comment on social and political issues.

senses.definition: a section of a newspaper or magazine that regularly publishes articles about a particular subject

examples.x: The sports column provides updates and commentary on local teams.
Her column focuses on tips for healthy living.
The magazine’s fashion column reviews the latest trends.
He writes a column about technology every month.
Each week, the newspaper features a political column by a well-known journalist.

senses.definition: a journalist who regularly writes articles on a particular subject for a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: The columnist wrote a compelling opinion piece on environmental conservation.
She is a fashion columnist known for her insights into the latest trends.
The newspaper hired a political columnist to cover the upcoming election.
The columnist's weekly column on technology reviews has a loyal following.
He is a sports columnist who analyzes games and player performances.

senses.definition: an individual who provides information

examples.x: Her source provided valuable insights into the company's financial status.
The source confirmed the details of the upcoming policy changes.
The journalist thanked the source for their cooperation.
Using multiple sources helps ensure the accuracy of the news report.

senses.definition: a newspaper article expressing the views of the editor on a particular subject

examples.x: The editorial argued for more investment in public transportation.
She wrote an editorial about the importance of voting in local elections.
His editorial criticized the government's handling of the economic crisis.
The latest editorial highlighted the need for healthcare reform.

senses.definition: a lengthy article in a newspaper or magazine, often written by a specialist about a certain topic

examples.x: The feature explored the impact of technology on education.
The magazine's latest issue includes a feature on sustainable living.
She wrote a feature about the rise of electric cars for the newspaper.
The travel magazine published a feature on hidden gems in Europe.
His feature on urban gardening received a lot of positive feedback.

senses.definition: a written or spoken description of an event, especially one that is intended to be broadcast or published

examples.x: The journalist's report was featured on the evening news.
The police report described the events of the accident.
She submitted a detailed report on the project's progress.

senses.definition: the activity or job of producing news stories for publication or broadcast

examples.x: The job of reporting involves gathering facts and presenting them clearly.
His career in reporting began at a small local newspaper.
Good reporting helps the public understand complex issues better.

senses.definition: an overall way in which something is changing or developing

examples.x: The current trend in fashion includes bold patterns and vibrant colors.
There is a growing trend towards remote work and flexible scheduling among companies.
The trend in smartphone technology shows an increasing emphasis on camera quality and AI features.
Economic analysts are observing a downward trend in consumer spending this quarter.
Social media platforms often influence trends in popular culture and communication styles.

senses.definition: having a close connection with the situation or subject at hand

examples.x: Her research findings were relevant to the current discussion on climate change.
He included only relevant information in his presentation to keep it focused.
The course covers topics that are relevant to today's business environment.
They had a relevant discussion about the implications of the new policy.
It's important to provide relevant examples to support your argument.

senses.definition: a written or spoken statement that proves the truth of something

examples.x: The email included a confirmation of the appointment time and date.
She waited for a confirmation before booking the flight.
The manager sent a confirmation that the meeting was scheduled for next week.
After submitting the form, you will receive a confirmation message on your phone.
The receipt served as a confirmation of the purchase.

senses.definition: in a state in which one is not informed about important things

examples.x: I'm completely in the dark about their plans for the weekend.
They kept us in the dark about the changes to the project until the last minute.
She prefers to work quietly and independently, often leaving her colleagues in the dark about her progress.
Without access to reliable news sources, they were left in the dark about recent events in the world.
The sudden power outage left everyone in the dark, groping for flashlights.
